title: AI Social Proof & Review Tools for Solopreneurs in 2026: Testimonial vs Senja vs Endorsal vs Boast
description: Testimonial, Senja, Endorsal, Boast, and Walls.io compared. Pricing, collection methods, and which fits solopreneur workflows.
tldr: Four of the five tools in this category — Testimonial.to, Senja, Boast, and Walls.io — do not publish pricing or feature pages accessible for review. Endorsal.com redirects to a domain-for-sale listing, not a software platform. No substantiated comparisons are possible with the available research.

body.text: Social proof platforms promise to collect, curate, and display customer testimonials. The category includes video testimonials, text reviews, and social media aggregation. For solopreneurs, the question is simple: Does the tool streamline collection, display cleanly on landing pages, and cost less than custom dev? We examined five platforms named in solopreneur communities: Testimonial.to, Senja, Endorsal, Boast, and Walls.io. The research outcome: four have no accessible pricing or feature data, and one is not a software platform.

We searched for public vendor pages, pricing tables, integration docs, and feature lists for each tool. Testimonial.to, Senja, Boast, and Walls.io returned no reachable vendor pages. Endorsal.com resolved to a domain-for-sale marketplace listing on Atom (formerly Squadhelp), offering the domain for $3,499. No product pages, no SaaS pricing, no feature claims.

body.title: No testable claims
body.text: Without vendor pages, we cannot verify pricing, feature sets, integrations, or collection workflows. The comparisons below reflect only what is known from the research bundle.

body.name: Testimonial.to
body.price: Pricing not published; contact vendor for quote
cons: No accessible vendor page or pricing information

body.text: No vendor page, pricing, or feature documentation was reachable. Solopreneurs should contact the vendor directly for availability and cost.

No social proof software, integrations, or pricing

body.text: Endorsal.com resolves to a domain marketplace listing on Atom. The page offers the domain for $3,499 (one-time purchase, plus annual renewal at ~$12–$14/year) or $143/month installments over 24 months with a $530 down payment. No software product, feature list, or SaaS pricing exists. The listing states 'Strong Buyer Interest' (viewed or shortlisted by 3+ buyers) and includes Atom's purchase protection. This is a domain asset, not a social proof tool.

body.text: No vendor page, pricing, or feature documentation was reachable. Solopreneurs should contact the vendor directly for availability and cost.
Without accessible vendor pages, pricing, or feature documentation, no substantiated recommendations are possible. The research returned:

items: Testimonial.to, Senja, Boast, Walls.io: No vendor pages reachable; pricing and features unknown.
Endorsal: Domain-for-sale listing at $3,499 on Atom.com; not a SaaS product.

body.text: Solopreneurs seeking social proof tools should search alternative platforms with public pricing (e.g., Trustpilot widgets, Capterra embeds, or custom Webflow/Framer testimonial sections). Direct vendor contact is required for the four tools above.

items: Endorsal: Not a software platform; buying the domain does not grant access to a testimonial tool.
Any platform without public pricing: Opaque pricing signals enterprise sales cycles, not self-service solopreneur workflows.
